name: Margaret Whiting
abstract: Margaret Eleanor Whiting (July 22, 1924 – January 10, 2011) was an American singer of popular music  who gained popularity in the 1940s and 1950s.

about_quotes.speaker: Annie Ross
about_quotes.evidence: I was reading Margaret Whiting’s notice from The Times, about dying. Her father was a great songwriter. She had a wonderful conception. She kept working. She was 86. I think she worked until she was 84 or something. Her father, boy did he write good songs. She was funny and strange.
about_quotes.doc_title: Annie Ross — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: I was recording with all the singers. I did so many albums behind Nat Cole, Margaret Whiting.

about_quotes.speaker: Bucky Pizzarelli
about_quotes.evidence: get a Jo Stafford record, Peggy Lee record, and Margaret Whiting. She was there too.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bucky Pizzarelli interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, May 23, 2003 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: He would have guests like Tony Bennett and Margaret Whiting, all singers.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
